MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Continued To Aug-13-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs DAVID KARNER and MICHAEL ROCHE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. The declaration of Mary Wiederhold establishes that she contacted Ms. Zahner to investigate the wrongful eviction and see whether others had complained about the property. Under Dove Audio, Inc. v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Sussman (1996), 47 Cal.App.4th 777, 781, "communications made during an attorney's investigatory interviews with private individuals preparatory to a hearing" implicate the anti-SLAPP statute and the litigation privilege. The burden has shifted to plaintiffs to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs fail to meet their burden. First, plaintiff merely rely on the declaration of counsel and fail to show that the statement was even uttered. Second, assuming that Ms. Wiederhold made the statement, it is privileged under CC 47 and Dove Audio. Third, assuming that the statement is one of fact, the only evidence before the court demonstrates that it is true. The declaration of Mr. Karner cites the habitability issues and the premises is fairly characterized as a "hovel." The court grants $5,652.50 in fees and costs to defendants Karner and Roche. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Smith, Esq., 415.294.1465, for defendant Mary Catherine Wiederhold DBA Law Office of Mary Catherine Wiederhold; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANT MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD DBA LAW OFFICE OF MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. The declaration of Mary Wiederhold establishes that she contacted Ms. Zahner to investigate the wrongful eviction and see whether others had complained about the property. Under Dove Audio, Inc. v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Sussman (1996), 47 Cal.App.4th 777, 781, "communications made during an attorney's investigatory interviews with private individuals preparatory to a hearing" implicate the anti-SLAPP statute and the litigation privilege. The burden has shifted to plaintiffs to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs fail to meet their burden. First, plaintiff merely rely on the declaration of counsel and fail to show that the statement was even uttered. Second, assuming that Ms. Wiederhold made the statement, it is privileged under CC 47 and Dove Audio. Third, assuming that the statement is one of fact, the only evidence before the court demonstrates that it is true. The declaration of Mr. Karner cites the habitability issues and the premises is fairly characterized as a "hovel." Defendant Wiederhold may seek her fees by motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.
